ogTitle: Swansea Heads vs Sweetmans Creek: Climate Safety

heading: Swansea Heads vs Sweetmans Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Swansea Heads, New South Wales and Sweetmans Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Swansea Heads has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Sweetmans Creek (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Swansea Heads or Sweetmans Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Swansea Heads has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Sweetmans Creek scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Swansea Heads scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Sweetmans Creek scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Swansea Heads or Sweetmans Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Swansea Heads has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
